title: Regional block follow-up documentation

treatment_and_management.text: Supports documented follow-up systems, mobilization status, discharge information, and postoperative education without directing management.
treatment_and_management.rationale: The exact RCoA recommendations support documenting follow-up after regional anesthesia, return of function, mobilization assessment, discharge process, education, and already arranged contact or review without asserting a normal block course.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Supports documented follow-up systems, mobilization status, discharge information, and postoperative education without directing management.

source.title: Guidelines for the Provision of Regional Anaesthesia Services 2025

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 8.41–8.43 — follow-up, mobilization, discharge, and education

sources_used.title: Guidelines for the Provision of Regional Anaesthesia Services 2025

additions.text: Supports documented follow-up systems, mobilization status, discharge information, and postoperative education without directing management.

source_limitations: Technique, site, time, expected duration, pain, sensory and motor function, weakness, mobility, falls, bladder function, catheter, wound, infection, systemic symptoms, examination, complication, action, response, escalation, discharge, and follow-up timing remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
